name: Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation
description: The Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation, known as Freddie Mac, is a government-sponsored enterprise (GSE) chartered by Congress in 1970 to support the U.S. housing finance system and to help ensure a reliable and affordable supply of mortgage funds throughout the country. Freddie Mac operates in the secondary mortgage market by purchasing residential mortgages from lenders, packaging them into mortgage-backed securities, and selling them to investors. This process provides liquidity, stability, and affordability to the nation's housing market.
